Off Calendar Dec-28-2012 Text Ruling

Appearances:
Joseph Sandoval, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3867, for defendant and moving party;
Michael Sweet, Esq. of Fox Rothschild, Tel. 415.364.5548, for plaintiff and opposing party.

The matter was argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motions: (1) To Compel Cypress Security LLP To Comply With Its Agreement To Produce Documents Responsive To The City's Second Document Request And For Monetary Sanctions (2) To Compel Cypress Security LLP To Produce At Its Deposition The Documents Responsive To The City'S Notice Of Deposition Of Cypress Security LLC And For Monetary Sanctions. The motions are granted but no sanctions are awarded. Judge Pro Tem: James Watson; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-18-2012 Set For Trial On Mar-04-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Mtn To Strike Portions; Req For Judicial Ntc
Off Calendar Apr-24-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Phillip F. Shinn of Fox Rothschild LLP (364.5558) for plaintiff and responding party; S. Anne Johnson of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3956) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court modified its ruling as follows: Denied. The determination of what is reasonable time to make a demand for payment is a factual question to be made on the particular circumstances of each case. The language of paragraph 100A indicates that defendant is responsible for payment in arrears throughout the term of the contract and paragraph 7 of the complaint alleges that the term of the contract was extended. Given these facts, the Court cannot conclude what, if any, claimed damages are time-barred. While paragraph 12 does not appear to be an attorney's fees clause, the clause is ambiguous and it would be premature to strike the prayer at this stage of the litigation. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976

Mtn To Strike Portions; Req For Judicial Ntc
